programmes.show_description: Ahh, the one hit wonder. They sneak up on us from nowhere, have one big hit and then never darken the UK top 20 again!
Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights & iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made the 80s!
Strap yourself in as Andy takes you back to late 80s teatime on BBC1 - Blue Peter, Grange Hill & Neighbours - plus 30 of the very best feel-good chart bangers from that era!
Dramatic storylines, big dance routines, neon lights and iconic fashions - these are the classic music videos that made 1988!
Sixty minutes of back to back UK chart runners-up from 1988 - a year when Stock, Aitken and Waterman ruled the roost! With Mr Charts himself, Mark Goodier.
Trying to impress someone with your cool moves? Give these a go, as we party through the day on NOW 80s.

programmes.show_name: Jenny Powell: Such A Good Feeling!
programmes.show_description: Dancing through history - one anthem at a time! From late 80s optimism to mid 90s euphoria, it's where house, rave and eurodance all started. Hands in the air people!
Trying to impress someone with your cool moves? Give these a go, as we party through the day on NOW 80s.
From Evelyn Thomas' High Energy in 1984, through Stock, Aitken and Waterman, and on to the breakout of happy house at the end of the decade - this massive dance session is full of bounce!

40 dancefloor classics - one from the '80s followed by another from the '70s. Simple!

Funk, soul, Hi-NRG, new jack swing and house took dance in all sorts of new directions during the '80s, and Radio 1's Friday night 'Big Beat' DJ Jeff Young was there!

programmes.show_name: So Awful, Yet So Amazing! Top 20
programmes.show_description: Twenty songs and videos from the '80s that some would think are pretty awful, but we think are just magnificent - in their own way! Please leave credibility shoes at the door.

Bruno Brookes has stories galore from 20 of rock and pop's real game changers. Icons, rebels, activists & a few of the more unexpected heroes who reshaped music & the media.

1988 was a top year for pop - and here's the proof!

Twenty hits from their '80s and early '90s pomp, including four UK number 1s. Beginning with West End Girls and running through It's a Sin, Always On My Mind, Go West and Absolutely Fabulous!
Who better to present a definitive countdown of 80s synthpop classics than OMD frontman Andy McCluskey? From Kraftwerk, Ultravox & Gary Numan to New Order, Yazoo and Pet Shop Boys.
Before the CD, the perfect pop songs were spun by singers and bands on 7 inch vinyl. From 1980-1984 these were the classic UK hits that invaded the world!
What was in the top five during the Easter holiday in the '80s? We choose three mega-hits from each year, 1980 to 1989, with Madonna, David Bowie, Bros, The Jam, Culture Club and more!
